# Codex Telegram Bridge v0.2.8

This release makes local file delivery predictable and hardens startup when the Codex app-server
is not ready during WSL boot.

## Highlights

- Resolves `/getfile` queries locally with `fd`/`fdfind`, including extension filters, hidden files,
  literal path fragments, safety validation, and paginated Telegram results.
- Prevents app-server startup races from reaching the Python bridge or producing repeated Telegram
  reconnect notices.
- Retries the managed Codex daemon after boot-time readiness failures and gates the bridge on its
  private control socket.
- Keeps installer, CI, systemd units, tests, and public bootstrap checksums synchronized.

## Upgrade notes

- Existing credentials, Telegram bindings, Codex state, session spaces, and installer-owned units
  are preserved by the v0.2.8 installer.
- The supported installer host remains Ubuntu or Debian under WSL2 with systemd and Python 3.14.
